channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Farmers fight battery storage proposals on agricultural land
item.description: Companies including Samsung are applying for planning permission to install three battery storage facilities on farmland in eastern Victoria.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kutchel

media:group.media:description: James Faithful wants planning approval denied for battery energy storage systems on farmland in west Gippsland.

item.title: Live: Greens open to CGT inquiry as party takes 'wait and see' approach to tax overhaul
item.description: The Greens leave the door open to sending the government's proposed changes to the capital gains tax (CGT) to an inquiry. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Senator, Nick McKim, speaking at an anti-salmon rally on Parliament Lawns in Hobart on 20 March, 2025.

item.title: Trump says 'not to rush into a deal' as potential Iran agreement emerges
item.description: Regional officials say the US is close to reaching a deal with Iran that would end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and see Iran give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium.

media:group.media:description: The deal could see the Strait of Hormuz reopened.

item.title: Manual cars 'a dying race' but new drivers are not giving them up
item.description: Roxsy Francis is full of nerves and excitement as she prepares to take her manual licence test, even as the cars drop in popularity.
item.dc:creator: Owen Jacques

media:group.media:description: Roxsy Francis says although friends have chosen to go for an auto licence, she wanted to try for a manual.

item.title: 'Killing me inside': The legacy of government policies and stolen kids
item.description: At nine years old, Bronwyn was sent to a notorious government-run children's home without her parents' consent. What unfolded next impacted the rest of her life.
item.dc:creator: Tahnee Jash

media:group.media:description: After years of pain and trauma, Stolen Generations Survivor, Aunty Bronwyn Smith shares her story for the first time.

item.title: With an Iran deal looming, Republicans are turning on each other
item.description: The US President says he "won't be rushed" in announcing the details, but already critics — and Iran — are suggesting he lost the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: ABC Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran in Jerusalem

item.title: 'More imaginative than Bays West': New waterfront Sydney suburb needs a name
item.description: In the coming weeks Sydneysiders will be asked to help rename Bays West, while a competition will open to decide the "look and feel" of the future suburb.
item.dc:creator: Nick Dole

media:group.media:description: The 77-hectare site on Sydney Harbour is earmarked for housing, green space, cultural venues and a working harbour.

item.title: Man charged with attempted murder and arson after double stabbing and house fire in Raceview
item.description: Police were called to an altercation at an Ipswich address on Sunday afternoon.

media:group.media:description: Police have charged a man following an alleged double stabbing and house fire in Ipswich.

item.title: Yasmin's 'heart sunk' when she heard the judge deliver her rapist's sentence
item.description: The Canberra woman says the justice system is unpredictable for victim-survivors but many aspects are worth it.
item.dc:creator: Tahlia Roy

media:group.media:description: Yasmin said she was shocked her offender avoided time behind bars.

item.title: Progressive governor of SA or 'ruthless' manipulator? Who was Grey?
item.description: Sir George Grey was the former governor of South Australia and is remembered as a "bundle of contradictions" in both Australia and New Zealand, where he played a key role in the Māori wars. But amid calls for the seat that bears his name to be renamed, who was Grey?
item.dc:creator: Luke Lay and Emma Pedler

media:group.media:description: South Australia's largest electorate is named after Sir George Grey, an explorer and former governor of the state with a complicated legacy.

item.title: First Australian activist detained from Gaza flotilla returns home
item.description: Gemma O'Toole is the first of 11 Australian activists detained by Israel off the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla to arrive back in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Nelli Saarinen

media:group.media:description: Gemma O'Toole was detained by Israeli forces when they intercepted the Global Sumud Flotilla off the coast of Cyprus.

item.title: Truro businesses call for certainty on freight bypass project
item.description: SA businesses situated along a major thoroughfare say they are holding back on making decisions about their future, amid uncertainty surrounding a slated road project that could divert traffic around the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Hunter

media:group.media:description: It is estimated the Greater Adelaide Freight Bypass project would divert up to 420,000 trucks a year around the city.

item.title: Coronial inquest into Porepunkah police shootings set to begin
item.description: Two separate directions hearings will be heard in the Coroners Court of Victoria before State Coroner Judge Liberty Sanger, who will oversee the structure and scope of the inquests.
item.dc:creator: Ashlee Aldridge

media:group.media:description: The hearing in the Coroners Court of Victoria will examine the deaths of slain Victoria Police officers Vadim De Waart-Hottart and Neal Thompson.

item.title: Quiz: Will you land a knockout punch on this week's history round?
item.description: Which is older — Alien or Star Wars? And which sea creature did the UK and Iceland go to war over?

media:group.media:description: Muhammad Ali stands over Sonny Liston after knocking him out during the world heavyweight boxing championship fight on May 26, 1965.

item.title: Australia's opening times are world leading and researchers say it's a strength
item.description: Australia has a "distinctive strength" when it comes to early trading, but more knowledge is needed for businesses to fully capitalise on it.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: Brisbane's South Bank is a hive of activity from the early hours.

item.title: Tottenham saves EPL status with win as West Ham goes down
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur salvages its Premier League status in the nick of time, with Joao Palhinha's first-half goal earning a priceless 1-0 home victory over Everton.

media:group.media:description: Tottenham Hotspur's Joao Palhinha celebrates the goal that kept his club in the English Premier League.

item.title: Top spy boss first witness as antisemitism royal commission resumes
item.description: The head of Australia's spy agency will be the first witness when the royal commission on antisemitism resumes this week.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Mike Burgess is among five witnesses due to give evidence during the second block of hearings at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

item.title: Trump's deal-making leaves Iran in the driver's seat and Israel more vulnerable
item.description: Israel goes from looking like the new regional hegemon to looking like it is under greater threat now than it has in years, while Iran now has room to rebuild its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Laura Tingle

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump is pushing for a negotiated end to the Iran war.

item.title: Head of Victoria's new childcare watchdog vows to protect kids
item.description: Former Victoria Police deputy commissioner Wendy Steendam assures parents the childcare system is safe, vowing to safeguard children following multiple cases of alleged abuse.
item.dc:creator: Richard Willingham
item.category: Early Childhood Education and Care Services

media:group.media:description: Wendy Steendam has vowed to improve the state's childcare system.

item.title: Scientist 'shocked' $300m irrigation scheme pushing ahead without studies
item.description: A $300 million irrigation scheme will supply 37.2 gigalitres of water to farming areas near Hobart from 2030. A scientists fears the taxpayer-funded scheme could put a major river at risk.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith

media:group.media:description: Scientist Christine Coughanowr says concerns raised in a 2002 study into the lower Derwent have not been disproved.

item.title: Why global conflict is reshaping Australian politics
item.description: US President Donald Trump was yesterday touting an end to the war in Iran, but declaring mission accomplished in Washington will not spare Australian consumers, and politicians, from the economic fallout of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Karvelas

media:group.media:description: As the war in Iran hits supermarket prices in Australia, more and more voters tired of the status quo wonder if One Nation has the answers.

item.title: Link between terrorism and DV could be key to preventing homicide
item.description: A world-first research project is aiming to prevent family and intimate partner homicides with similar strategies to those being used to tackle terrorism and lone-actor attacks.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Gleeson

media:group.media:description: People run with their hands up from the Lindt Cafe, Martin Place during a hostage standoff on December 16, 2014 in Sydney

item.title: Turkish police raid opposition party HQ, fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: The raid marks the end of a three-day stand-off between members of the opposition CHP party and police.

media:group.media:description: Police were seen entering the opposition Republican People's Party (CHP) headquarters in Ankara on Sunday, local time.

item.title: The once-promising AFL player hitting home runs in US Major League
item.description: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Day

media:group.media:description: A promising AFL player when he was younger, Curtis Mead had to choose between Australian football and baseball.

item.title: Not moving jobs, states or starting businesses: Data says Australians are stuck
item.description: People are less mobile, less dynamic and more risk-averse than we used to be, a series of economic indicators shows.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ziffer

media:group.media:description: Kate McCready worries workers are in for trouble ahead after decades of consistent economic growth.
